title: crepidomanes proliferum
intro: Crepidomanes proliferum, also known as the forked spleenwort, is a type of epiphytic fern native to tropical regions of the world. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of various phenolic compounds and flavonoids. The health benefits of Crepidomanes proliferum include its potential to help manage fever, reduce inflammation, and improve immune function, making it a popular ingredient in traditional remedies. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used as an infusion or decoction, or combined with other herbs to create teas, tinctures, and topical treatments.
